25.reason: [Visited should be marked when a vertex is enqueued, not when popped. This can cause duplicate enqueues and incorrect BFS order.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Visited should be marked when a vertex is enqueued, not when popped. This can cause duplicate enqueues and incorrect BFS order.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Checking visited status upon dequeuing is incorrect for BFS; it should be marked upon enqueuing to prevent duplicate entries in the queue.

32.reason: [Error: Should mark visited[j]=1 when enqueuing to prevent duplicate nodes from being added.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Visited should be set to 1 immediately after enqueuing to avoid duplicates. Missing visited[j]=1.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: Should mark visited[j]=1 when enqueuing to prevent duplicate nodes from being added.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Error: Missing 'visited[j]=1;' here. In BFS, a vertex must be marked as visited immediately when enqueued to avoid processing it multiple times.

38.reason: [Variable order swapped: first input (vertices) is stored in m, second (edges) in n. This is counterintuitive but consistent with loops. However, it may cause confusion.]

21.reason: [Error: Condition should be head < tail because tail points to next free slot. Using <= will cause an extra iteration accessing invalid queue element.]

29.reason: Error: Marking visited here (on dequeue) is incorrect. Vertices should be marked visited when they are added to the queue.
